name: Plaits
description: Digital macro-oscillator with 16 synthesis models. Built-in low-pass gate and decaying envelope allow self-contained percussive and melodic voices without external modules.

controls.name: Model Selection Buttons
controls.description: Two buttons cycle through 8+8 synthesis models. Bank 1: pitched/tonal. Bank 2: noisy/percussive.
controls.detail: Short press: cycle within bank · Long press: switch bank · LEDs indicate active model

controls.name: Frequency
controls.description: Coarse pitch control. Default range 8 octaves (C0–C8); narrowable to ±7 semitones.
controls.detail: Hold button 2 + turn HARMONICS to set range · C0–C8 default

controls.name: Harmonics
controls.description: Model-dependent tone control. Generally adjusts harmonic content, chord type, or waveform.
controls.detail: Function varies per model · CV controllable

controls.name: Timbre
controls.description: Model-dependent tone control. Generally sweeps spectral content from dark/sparse to bright/dense.
controls.detail: Has dedicated attenuverter for FM/envelope · CV controllable

controls.name: Morph
controls.description: Model-dependent tone control. Explores lateral timbral variations and waveform transitions.
controls.detail: Has dedicated attenuverter · LPG decay time in hold mode · CV controllable

controls.name: FM / Timbre / Morph Attenuverters
controls.description: Three small attenuverters for the FM, Timbre, and Morph CV inputs. Also scale internal envelope amount.
controls.detail: CCW: invert · noon: zero · CW: unity · Acts as envelope depth when TRIG patched

inputs.name: Model CV
inputs.description: Voltage selects synthesis model. Two LEDs indicate modulated vs. base value.

inputs.name: Timbre CV
inputs.description: CV input for Timbre parameter, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: FM
inputs.description: CV input for frequency modulation, scaled by FM attenuverter.

inputs.name: Morph CV
inputs.description: CV input for Morph parameter, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: Trigger
inputs.description: Triggers internal decaying envelope and LPG. Excites physical/percussive models. Samples MODEL CV.

inputs.name: Level
inputs.description: Opens internal low-pass gate directly. Acts as accent when triggering physical models.

inputs.name: V/Oct
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch input. Controls frequency relative to FREQUENCY knob root note.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Main audio output of the active synthesis model.

outputs.name: AUX
outputs.description: Auxiliary output: variant, sidekick, or by-product of the main signal depending on model.

behaviors.name: Classic Waveforms / Waveshaping (Models 1–2)
behaviors.description: Model 1: virtual-analog pair (pulse/saw). Model 2: asymmetric triangle through waveshaper and wavefolder. HARMONICS selects waveform, TIMBRE folds/shapes, MORPH varies asymmetry.

behaviors.name: FM / Formant / Harmonic (Models 3–5)
behaviors.description: Model 3: two-op FM with feedback/chaos. Model 4: granular formant simulation via sine segments. Model 5: additive harmonic mixture. HARMONICS controls ratio/count, TIMBRE shapes content.

behaviors.name: Wavetable / Chords / Speech (Models 6–8)
behaviors.description: Model 6: 4 banks of 8×8 wavetables with interpolation. Model 7: four-note chords via VA or wavetable. Model 8: vowel and LPC speech synthesis. HARMONICS selects bank/chord/vowel type.

behaviors.name: Granular / Filtered Noise / Particle (Models 9–11, bank 2)
behaviors.description: Model 9: 8 enveloped sawtooth grains. Model 10: variable-clock white noise through resonant filter. Model 11: dust noise through allpass/bandpass networks. Percussive and textural sources.

behaviors.name: Internal LPG
behaviors.description: Built-in low-pass gate. TIMBRE adjusts character (VCFA to VCA). MORPH sets decay time. Active on tonal models when TRIG patched.

behaviors.name: LFO Mode
behaviors.description: Drops to 0.015–16Hz range. All 16 models become complex LFO shapes. OUT and AUX give different waveshapes from the same model. Model 9 in LFO mode produces two non-repeating random CV streams.

behaviors.name: MODEL CV latching
behaviors.description: MODEL CV is only sampled on trigger edges — the model does not change mid-note. A slow CV sweep paired with a rhythmic trigger produces beat-synced model switching rather than continuous morphing.

behaviors.name: Internal envelope normalization
behaviors.trigger: TRIG patched with CV inputs unpatched
behaviors.description: When TRIG is patched, TIMBRE, FM, and MORPH CV inputs are normalled to the internal decaying envelope. The three attenuverters then control how much envelope depth is applied to each parameter. Patching any CV cable into those jacks breaks the normalling for that parameter only.

behaviors.name: Color-blind mode
behaviors.trigger: power on while holding right button
behaviors.description: Toggles LED behavior to use brightness levels instead of green/red color coding for the two model banks. Useful in low-light environments or for performers with color vision deficiency.

behaviors.name: Frequency range narrowing
behaviors.description: Default pitch range is 8 octaves. Turning HARMONICS CCW while holding right button narrows the FREQUENCY knob range down to ±7 semitones, enabling fine chromatic tuning without an external attenuverter on the knob.

patchIdeas.name: 808-style kick drum
patchIdeas.patch: Model 14 (bass drum) · HARMONICS at 9 o'clock (low tuning) · TIMBRE at 9 o'clock (soft click) · MORPH at 10 o'clock (medium decay) · short trigger from sequencer · AUX = distorted variation for layering
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
* Model: 14 (bass drum)
- Sequencer (Trig) t> Plaits (Trigger)
- Plaits (OUT) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Plaits (AUX) -> Mixer (In 2) // distorted layer

patchIdeas.name: DX7 electric piano
patchIdeas.patch: Model 4 (Plaits formant bank, select keyboard FM preset) · HARMONICS turned to EP harmonic preset position · TIMBRE at 70% (moderate FM index) · MORPH short (tight decay) · V/Oct from keyboard · internal LPG active · clean playable FM piano voice from one module
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
* Model: 4 (FM)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Plaits (V/Oct)
- Keyboard (Gate) g> Plaits (Trigger) // activates internal LPG
- Plaits (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Drum machine from one module
patchIdeas.patch: MODEL CV from 3-step sequencer outputting 0V / 0.31V / 0.62V · TRIG from clock · 0V = kick (Model 14) · 0.31V = snare (Model 15) · 0.62V = hi-hat (Model 16) · sequence all three drum voices with one Plaits and one sequencer
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
- Sequencer (CV Out) >> Plaits (Model CV) // 0/0.31/0.62V selects kick/snare/hat
- Clock (Out) t> Plaits (Trigger)
- Plaits (OUT) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Chiptune arpeggiator
patchIdeas.patch: Model 8 (speech/LPC bank, orange) · clock → TRIG · TIMBRE swept to select phoneme/arpeggio mode · AUX = NES-style triangle wave bass line · mix OUT + AUX for two independent voices from one module
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
* Model: 8 (speech/LPC)
- Clock (Out) c> Plaits (Trigger)
- Plaits (OUT) -> Mixer (In 1) // arpeggio lead
- Plaits (AUX) -> Mixer (In 2) // triangle bass

patchIdeas.name: Granular ambient CV
patchIdeas.patch: Model 9 · hold right button + HARMONICS full CCW to enter LFO mode · HARMONICS at 2 o'clock (wide pitch spread across grains) · OUT and AUX = two non-repeating, slowly wandering random CV sources · patch to filter FM and reverb send for ambient self-generating textures
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
* Model: 9 (LFO mode)
- Plaits (OUT) >> Filter (FM CV) // random wandering CV
- Plaits (AUX) >> Reverb (Send CV) // independent second stream

patchIdeas.name: Self-patch feedback FM
patchIdeas.patch: Model 3 (2-op FM) · OUT → MORPH CV input via attenuverter set at 10 o'clock · audio-rate feedback creates metallic, chaotic tones · TIMBRE controls carrier/modulator ratio · HARMONICS adjusts base FM depth · sweep MORPH attenuverter for feedback amount control
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
* Model: 3 (2-op FM)
- Plaits (OUT) >> Plaits (Morph CV) // audio-rate self feedback
- Plaits (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bowed string drone
patchIdeas.patch: Model 12 (modal resonator/string) · no trigger patched · MORPH at 3 o'clock (long resonance) · V/Oct from keyboard or fixed pitch CV · LEVEL input from slow LFO for breathing bow pressure · mix OUT + AUX for stereo spread
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
* Model: 12 (modal)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Plaits (V/Oct)
- LFO (Out) >> Plaits (Level) // breathing bow pressure
- Plaits (OUT) -> Mixer (L)
- Plaits (AUX) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Supersaw / unison lead
patchIdeas.patch: Model 9 (sawtooth grains) · HARMONICS at 9 o'clock (tight unison) · TIMBRE full CW (bright, dense) · V/Oct from sequencer · TRIG from gate · mix OUT + AUX (slightly detuned grain clusters) for wide supersaw sound
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
* Model: 9 (grains)
- Sequencer (CV) p> Plaits (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Plaits (Trigger)
- Plaits (OUT) -> Mixer (L)
- Plaits (AUX) -> Mixer (R) // detuned grain cluster

patchIdeas.name: Talking bass
patchIdeas.patch: Model 8 (speech synthesis) · MORPH positioned at formant vowel transition · V/Oct set for bass register (0V–1V) · slow LFO on TIMBRE CV at 1 o'clock attenuverter · MORPH CV from envelope for vowel-on-note effect · rhythmic gate triggers for consonant attacks
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
* Model: 8 (speech)
- Sequencer (CV) p> Plaits (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Plaits (Trigger)
- LFO (Out) >> Plaits (Timbre CV)
- Envelope (Out) >> Plaits (Morph CV) // vowel-on-note
- Plaits (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Beat-synced model switching
patchIdeas.patch: Slow ascending CV ramp (0–5V over 8 bars) → MODEL CV · clock → TRIG · model only changes on each trigger so switching is always in-time · HARMONICS and TIMBRE stay fixed, timbre character evolves bar by bar as model index advances
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plaits:
- LFO (Ramp) >> Plaits (Model CV) // 0-5V over 8 bars
- Clock (Out) c> Plaits (Trigger) // samples Model CV on edge
- Plaits (OUT) -> Output (In)
